tool_input.summary:
Force balance answer (from Juno-era dawnside magnetodisk analyses): the dominant *inward* force is the magnetic curvature/tension (J×B tension) associated with bent magnetodisk field lines; the dominant *outward* force is the centrifugal force from rapidly corotating plasma (with plasma pressure-gradient forces typically secondary).

Relevant PDS datasets to reproduce/verify this with Juno data are listed below (MAG/FGM for B-field → magnetic tension; JADE moments for density/velocity → centrifugal & pressure terms; SPICE for geometry; Waves density as a supplementary density product).

candidates.title: JNO J 3 FGM CAL V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Juno Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) calibrated magnetic-field time series needed to estimate magnetic stresses (magnetic tension / J×B) in the magnetodisk.

candidates.title: JNO SS 3 FGM CAL V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Companion Juno FGM calibrated dataset (cruise/spacecraft-segment) that can help provide magnetic-field context and coverage beyond the prime Jupiter-orbit segments, depending on interval of interest.

candidates.title: JNO J JAD 5 MOMENTS V1.0
candidates.reasoning: JADE plasma moments provide density, bulk flow, and temperature/pressure needed to compute outward centrifugal force (ρ v_φ^2 / r) and compare against magnetic forces in the dawnside magnetodisk.

candidates.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities from Characteristic Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Derived electron densities from Waves can supplement/validate density estimates used in force-balance terms (e.g., centrifugal and pressure-gradient contributions), especially where particle moments are limited.

candidates.title: JUNO SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels provide spacecraft trajectory/attitude and coordinate frames to place observations in local time (dawnside) and compute radial directions and centrifugal terms.

JADE moments for density/velocity → centrifugal & pressure terms; SPICE for geometry; Waves density as a supplementary density product).","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"JNO-J-3-FGM-CAL-V1.0","path":null,"title":"JNO J 3 FGM CAL V1.0","mission":"Juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Juno Fluxgate Magnetometer (FGM) calibrated magnetic-field time series needed to estimate magnetic stresses (magnetic tension / J×B) in the magnetodisk."},{"dataset_id":"JNO-SS-3-FGM-CAL-V1.0","path":null,"title":"JNO SS 3 FGM CAL V1.0","mission":"Juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Companion Juno FGM calibrated dataset (cruise/spacecraft-segment) that can help provide magnetic-field context and coverage beyond the prime Jupiter-orbit segments, depending on interval of interest."},{"dataset_id":"JNO-J-JAD-5-MOMENTS-V1.0","path":null,"title":"JNO J JAD 5 MOMENTS V1.0","mission":"Juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"JADE plasma moments provide density, bulk flow, and temperature/pressure needed to compute outward centrifugal force (ρ v_φ^2 / r) and compare against magnetic forces in the dawnside magnetodisk."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density::1.0","path":null,"title":"Juno Waves Electron Densities from Characteristic Plasma Wave Frequencies","mission":"Juno","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Derived electron densities from Waves can supplement/validate density estimates used in force-balance terms (e.g., centrifugal and pressure-gradient contributions), especially where particle moments are limited."},{"dataset_id":"JNO-J/E/SS-SPICE-6-V1.0","path":null,"title":"JUNO SPICE FILES","mission":"Juno","node":"naif","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"SPICE kernels provide spacecraft trajectory/attitude and coordinate frames to place observations in local time (dawnside) and compute radial directions and centrifugal terms."}]}
